title: Xanthocephalum grande
taxonomy: Xanthocephalum grande is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Xanthocephalum, which is part of the Heliantheae tribe. The plant is native to South America and is also known as the large yellowhead. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant is an annual herb, producing yellow daisy-like flowers.

intro: Xanthocephalum grande, also known as yellowtop, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore eyes, and inflammation, due to its reported analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Xanthocephalum grande include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, yellowtop is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
